MEASURE WHAT MATTERSвЂ”AND GET PAID FOR IT

Token spend is climbing everywhere https://every.. Some of that AI use is valuable; some isnвЂ™t. The only way to tell the difference and spend efficiently is to measureвЂ”but measure what?

Getting AI tools into your teamвЂ™s hands was part one of AI adoption. Today, companies must identify business-specific, measurable goals. That part, too, requires both a definition of вЂњgoodвЂќ and the data to measure how close youвЂ™re getting to it. Meanwhile, frontier labs are under pressure to improve at economically valuable domains such as finance, life sciences, and general reasoning. TheyвЂ™re paying for data that helps them get there. Well-defined goals now create value for your company in two ways: return on investment for you and your customers and data that can help others improve, too.

Over the past year at Good Start Labs https://every., weвЂ™ve built benchmarks, trained agents, and helped game publishers operationalize and monetize their data for that lab market. Arkadium https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vZGVsIElzIHRoZSBFYXN5IFBhcnQiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ3LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cDovL2Fya2FkaXVtLmNvbS8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0= is one publisher with hundreds of games played by tens of millions of players. We supported its recent launch of Game Lab https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vZGVsIElzIHRoZSBFYXN5IFBhcnQiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ3LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9nYW1lbGFiLmNvbS8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=, a public leaderboard scoring how well frontier models play simple games, in partnership with Meta and DeepMind. Arkadium set a clear goal: Give its players a good game against AI. Together we built the benchmarks and evaluated them against real users. Then the scores came in. The same models that make novel discoveries https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vZGVsIElzIHRoZSBFYXN5IFBhcnQiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ3LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuYWkuY29tL2luZGV4L2FjY2VsZXJhdGluZy1zY2llbmNlLWdwdC01LyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMH0= in math and science lose 90 percent of their Gin Rummy gamesвЂ”against casual players.

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vZGVsIElzIHRoZSBFYXN5IFBhcnQiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ3LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ2FtZWxhYi5jb20vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ==Opus canвЂ™t beat the daily crossword. (Image courtesy of gamelab.com.)




ThatвЂ™s because these models are shaped by what theyвЂ™ve seen: lots of math, and almost no Gin Rummy. Popular AI models may not have experience with whatever you work on all day either. But defining the goal revealed options for Arkadium beyond a large language modelвЂ”different forms of AI work well for different goals. Instead of an LLM, we trained an expert model with 4.6 million parameters in an 18-megabyte file that runs on a regular CPU. At full strength, it beats human players about 90 percent of the time, and the economics are as lopsided in its favor: At 1 million requests a day, our expert model would cost about $60 a year; a frontier LLM would run into the multi-millions.

For Arkadium, that вЂњgood gameвЂќ goal paid twice: a better experience for its players and a new revenue line in the form of selling anonymous data to labs for millions. Frontier models performed poorly at games; ArkadiumвЂ™s well-structured gameplay data from вЂњgoodвЂќ games with вЂњgoodвЂќ human players was what the labs needed to improve. As Arkadium continues to sell that anonymized data to frontier labs, its models will learn from it and apply that intelligence. (GPT-5.6 is already a much better cruciverbalist https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vZGVsIElzIHRoZSBFYXN5IFBhcnQiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ3LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly94LmNvbS9nb29kc2lkZS9zdGF0dXMvMjA3NjY5OTA2MTQ0OTU2ODU4Mz9zPTIwIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ==.)

Reddit, Shutterstock, and News Corp have already turned their data into hundreds of millions of dollars https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vZGVsIElzIHRoZSBFYXN5IFBhcnQiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ3LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9wbGF5dGVzdGluZy90aGUtbWFya2V0LWZvci1tYWtpbmctYWktYmV0dGVyIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEzfQ== in recurring revenue. TL;DR: WeвЂ™re announcing gift links, which let paid and All Access Every members share paywalled articles with anyone. Using Codex and the latest frontier models, senior editor Jack Cheng took on the project himself. The process showed us that people across Every can now build and test ideas without taking engineers away from more important work.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9hY2NvdW50IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



Last month, we rolled out gift links on the Every website. If you have a paid or All Access https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9vbi1ldmVyeS9pbnRyb2R1Y2luZy1ldmVyeS1hbGwtYWNjZXNzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9 membership, you can now share paywalled articles with people who arenвЂ™t Every members. Standard paid members can share five gift links each month, with the count resetting on the first of the month. All Access members can, starting today, share unlimited gift links.

Gift links by themselves arenвЂ™t novel. YouвЂ™ve likely interacted with them in articles in the online editions of the Wall Street Journal, the Atlantic, or the New York Times. But those publications also have product teams larger than our entire company of 30 people.

Many of us at Every are veterans of organizations structured to dampen risk and dodge uncertainty, often at the cost of experimentation. WeвЂ™re learning to explore new workflows https://every., cede control to AI agents when it makes sense https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9ndWlkZXMvdGhlLWVpZ2h0LWxldmVscy1vZi1haS1hZG9wdGlvbiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ==, and embrace the fact that good ideas can come from anyone https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9jb250ZXh0LXdpbmRvdy9jb2RleC1mb3ItZXZlcnl0aGluZy1hbmQtZXZlcnlvbmUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9вЂ”and now be built by anyone.

The path our small editorial team charted to ship the new gift links feature is a case study in how AI tools can turn a heavy organizational lift into a feasible experiment, and how an AI-native company decides what to build.

HereвЂ™s how we did it.


SCRATCHING MY OWN ITCH

As a senior editor at Every, IвЂ™ve edited dozens of pieces by our team and outside contributors, as well as written my own essays on creativity https://every., taste https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9wL3doYXQtaXMtdGFzdGUtcmVhbGx5IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ==, and maintenance https://every.. IвЂ™m proud of our work. I regularly share links to it in my personal newsletter https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9qYWNrY2hlbmcuY29tL3N1bmRheSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNH0=.

When I share articles from other paid publications, though, I usually use gift links. Some of my favorite blogs, like Metafilter https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWV0YWZpbHRlci5jb20vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE1fQ== or Jason Kottke https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IFdlIEJ1aWx0IEdpZnQgTGlua3MiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ2LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9rb3R0a2Uub3JnIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE2fQ==вЂ™s, tend to share paywalled articles as gift links as well. As much as I wanted gift links for myself, I saw a business case for them. Were we limiting EveryвЂ™s reach and potential for virality by not giving people a way to share paywalled content?

To find out, I started digging.

I first searched EveryвЂ™s Slack and Discord channels to see if anyone had raised the idea before. Maybe weвЂ™d even tried gift links in the

Viktor is an AI employee that works inside Slack and Microsoft Teams. It connects to 3,200+ tools you already use, runs the work end to end, and posts the finished result back in the channel. No dashboards to check, no new app to learn.

40,000+ teams have already hired one. Alpha Signal, one of the biggest AI newsletters, automated 18 workflows in 67 days with no developer. Hampton budgeted $440K for three hires; 44 days after installing Viktor, none of those hires were on the calendar.

No demo calls, no onboarding project. Start free with $100 in credits, no credit card needed.

The update moves Google closer to turning Search into an AI assistant that can complete everyday tasks instead of simply returning links. ([Read the announcement](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/search/connected-apps))

**3/ Roblox wants AI to build your next game:** The company unveiled [Build](https://about.roblox.com/newsroom/2026/07/build-without-limits-on-roblox), an AI-powered creation tool that turns text prompts into playable 3D games complete with environments, characters, gameplay mechanics, visuals, and sound. Users can refine projects in Roblox Studio before publishing, marking a major step toward making game development accessible to millions of creators. ([Read the announcement](https://about.roblox.com/newsroom/2026/07/build-without-limits-on-roblox))

Atlassian's new Jira capabilities centralize planning, orchestrating, and scaling agentic work across the entire software development life cycle. Assign tasks to Claude, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot directly from Jira (with Codex coming soon). Your work stays grounded in Jira, a single source of truth, providing context for agent responses.

Gain complete agent visibility locally and in the cloud with AtlassianвЂ™s Teamwork Graph**. **Store permission-aware session records, all while Teamwork Graph CLI hooks continuously update Jira with local session context to keep your teams aligned.

Increase productivity and accelerate planning with other new tools, too. Jira for Slack turns conversations into specs or assigns tasks. AI Planner drives spec-driven development in Confluence. And the Jira Coding Agent uses enterprise context and code intelligence to turn work items into ready-to-review pull requests, enabling fixes directly within Jira.

Assign work to any agent. Measure the impact. [Learn more](https://fandf.co/4wHy7iP).

The company says Inkling rivals leading open models while using significantly fewer tokens, giving developers a powerful foundation they can fully customize and self-host.

**2/ OpenAI just launched its first AI hardware device:** The company unveiled[ Codex Micro](https://openai.com/supply/co-lab/work-louder), a $230 programmable keypad built with Work Louder that lets developers control coding agents through dedicated keys, a joystick, and customizable commands. While designed for a niche audience, the launch offers an early glimpse into OpenAI's broader hardware ambitions ahead of its upcoming consumer AI devices.

SIGNAL

SKILLS COULD BE MAKING YOUR AI WORSE

It might be time to examine your elaborate skill library. Skills are reusable packages of instructionsвЂ”and sometimes examples and toolsвЂ”that load whenever theyвЂ™re relevant to what you asked your AI to do. TheyвЂ™re supposed to improve an agentвЂ™s performance. The format, popularized by Anthropic https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9hbnRocm9waWMuc2tpbGxqYXIuY29tL2ludHJvZHVjdGlvbi10by1hZ2VudC1za2lsbHMiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Nn0=, is all over X, where sprawling custom skill libraries are treated as status symbols https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly94LmNvbS9oZXluYXZ0b29yL3N0YXR1cy8yMDM2ODYxMjgwODU5MTI0MTAwIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9.

Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AbWlrZV8yMTE0IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjh9, EveryвЂ™s head of tech consulting, thinks every skill should earn its place in your library with proof it improves outcomes. His argument: Frontier models are smart enough that theyвЂ™ve absorbed the need for most of the skills trending on social media. If a model can reason through something on its ownвЂ”and Fable 5 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9zZWFyY2g_cXVlcnk9ZmFibGUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= or GPT-5.6 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9jaGFpbi1vZi10aG91Z2h0L2hvdy1ncHQtNS02LWNoYW5nZXMta25vd2xlZGdlLXdvcmsiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9 likely canвЂ”adding extra instructions creates confusion, not clarity. вЂњYouвЂ™re fighting the weights of the model by forcing it to do things your way instead of the way it was trained,вЂќ Mike says. Any time you conflict with the modelвЂ™s training, itвЂ™s more likely to make mistakes. All the additional text loaded from your skill will also inflate costs, so you should make sure each skill you choose is worth it.

Skills https://every. are still useful, Mike says, but only when you need the model to complete a workflow in a specific way, like producing a custom PowerPoint template https://every. with instructions on your brand style guide or referencing internal company data. Mike recently found when testing Fable 5 that some of the skills Opus 4.8 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by92aWJlLWNoZWNrL29wdXMtNC04LXZpYmVjaGVjayIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30= needed to avoid mistakes actually harmed the newer modelвЂ™s performance https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by92aWJlLWNoZWNrL2FudGhyb3BpYy1teXRob3Mtb3VyLWZhYmxlLXZpYmUtY2hlY2siLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTR9. It reminded Mike of his work as a prompt engineer in 2023: вЂњWith GPT-3 we had to use all these hacks and magic words to get it to produce valid code. Then when GPT-4 came out, it followed instructions better, and our bag of tricks was no longer necessary.вЂќ

WHY IT MATTERS:

The data backs him up. SWE-Skills-Bench https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIENhc2UgQWdhaW5zdCBTa2lsbHMiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ0LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9hcnhpdi5vcmcvYWJzLzI2MDMuMTU0MDEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTV9, a research benchmark that tests whether agent skills make agents better at software engineering, tested 49 public software-engineering skills and found that 39 didnвЂ™t impact performance, while three made things worse. At the same time, a large percentage of skills caused the model to consume more compute without improving results. (The worst offender increased token use by 451 percent.)

AI models have never been smarter https://every. or more capable https://every.. But using them to their full potential still requires AI skills, fluency, and most of all, the willingness to hand over complex work to an agentвЂ”or agents. Today, we show how our business operations team works across Fable https://every., Codex https://every., and the customer support platform Fin, then look at GranolaвЂ™s plan to turn meetings into context any agent can use. We also explain why Sonnet 5 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3VyZiB0aGUgTW9kZWxzIHdpdGggRXZlcnnigJlzIEJpeiBPcHMgVGVhbSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzNDMsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3ZpYmUtY2hlY2svc29ubmV0LTUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= still isnвЂ™t great at writing (but does better at more tightly scoped tasks), examine three roles emerging in the AI era, and share some of our favorite people to follow on X.

BIZ OPS AT THE FRONTIER

Executive operations manager Jalaiyah Bolden https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3VyZiB0aGUgTW9kZWxzIHdpdGggRXZlcnnigJlzIEJpeiBPcHMgVGVhbSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzNDMsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5saW5rZWRpbi5jb20vaW4vamFsYWl5YWgtYm9sZGVuLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0= had a couple of days to turn around a customer-support plan for the launch of Every All Access https://every., a new annual membership for builders that includes unlimited access to Every products, among other perks https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3VyZiB0aGUgTW9kZWxzIHdpdGggRXZlcnnigJlzIEJpeiBPcHMgVGVhbSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzNDMsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2J1aWxkZXItcGFjaz9zb3VyY2U9cG9zdF9idXR0b24iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTN9. The information she needed was scattered across half a dozen sources. вЂњIt was information overload,вЂќ she saysвЂ”the kind that could have sunk much bigger ops teams.

Instead of scrambling, Jalaiyah took a deep breath and surfed the models https://every..

https://every.вЂ™s initial Fable prompt. (Screenshot courtesy of Jalaiyah Bolden.)




First, she summoned Fable. Giving the model access to all relevant context, she instructed it to ask clarifying questions about anything that was missing, and then get to work.
